mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where FEDFUNDS declines, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening aggregate demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or out-of-sample hit rate < 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in real GDP growth and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer and business demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields negative 60d returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain utilization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when macro regime shifts to late-cycle or recession, or 5-day returns are negative in >55% of expansion-phase triggers.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate robust consumer confidence and construction sector demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as a leading indicator of economic momentum.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where HOUST increases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns (hit rate < 50%) or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index increase exceeding 0.3 percent signals accelerating inflation that forces monetary tightening expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60d returns in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.3%, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales growth reflects strengthening consumer spending and near-term corporate revenue momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during stable growth periods.
failure_condition: Signal reverses direction (predicts negative returns) during volatile or recessionary regimes, or 5-day hit rate drops below 45% in out-of-sample testing.
